kartikeya-ashtottara-shatanamavali: Perfect for archana worship of Lord Kartikeya with 108 names
Invokes victory over enemies and obstacles
Invokes the blessing of courage, wisdom, and divine knowledge
Traditionally recited for removal of diseases and sins
Recited on Skanda Shashthi and Tuesdays for the war-god's grace
kartikeya-hridayam: Heart hymn of Lord Kartikeya, commander of divine armies
Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies and obstacles with shakti power
Invokes victory, courage, and leadership qualities
Invokes Kartikeya as Jnana-Shakti and Guru
Chanted on Skanda Shashthi and Tuesdays by Murugan devotees
kartikeya-mangalashtakam: Eight verses calling down Murugan's auspicious mangala blessings
Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies and negative forces
Invokes courage, valor, and victory in endeavors
Traditionally recited for removal of diseases and bestows health
Sung on Skanda Shashthi and Kartikeya Jayanti for prosperity
kartikeya-pancharatnam: Five-gem hymn distilling devotion to the warrior-god Kartikeya
Draws down Murugan's grace as son of Shiva and Parvati
Calls on the spear-bearing god's shield against foes
Sung to clear inner faults and ripen Skanda's spiritual merit
Chanted on Skanda Shashthi and Tuesdays for the commander's favour
ketu-stotram: Five verses appeasing Ketu, the shadowy karmic node
Turns Ketu's force toward moksha and spiritual detachment
Calms the disturbances of an afflicted Ketu in the chart
Recited to ease Ketu's doshas and restore graha shanti
Ideal for Ketu graha shanti and worship
khatu-shyam-aarti: Invokes the blessings of Khatu Shyam Baba - deity of Kalyug
Provides hope and support to the hopeless - 'Haare Ka Sahara'
Traditionally recited for removal of diseases, sorrows, and all difficulties
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of heartfelt desires of devotees
Sung at the Phalguna Mela aarti at Khatu Dham
khatu-shyam-chalisa: Known as the deity of Kalyug - fulfills wishes of the downtrodden
Provides support to those who have lost all hope - 'Haare Ka Sahara'
Resolves marriage delays, job issues, and financial problems
Invokes justice and supports those who are truthful
Recited through the Phalguna Mela pilgrimage at Khatu Dham
krishna-ashtakam: Eight verses said to wash away sins of countless lifetimes
Invokes the blessings and vision of lord krishna
Traditionally associated with peace and purity of mind
Ideal for morning recitation for spiritual upliftment
Traditionally recited for removal of karmic obstacles from the devotee's path
krishna-ashtottara-shatanamavali: Reciting 108 names of Krishna bestows divine love and devotion
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins and grants liberation through krishna's grace
Used during Krishna Janmashtami puja and daily archana
Invokes wisdom, prosperity, and all forms of happiness
Chanted on Janmashtami, Govardhan Puja, and Holi for Gopala's grace
krishna-gayatri: Two Krishna Gayatri variations — Devakinandana and Govinda forms
Invokes intense krishna bhakti and divine love
Leads to Goloka — Krishna's supreme abode
Japa on Janmashtami, Wednesdays, and Ekadashi draws Krishna's grace
Fills the heart with devotion and bliss
Beautiful dhyanam captures the complete form of Krishna
krishna-gitavali-key-verses: Tulsidas's verses kindle tender love for the cowherd Krishna
Traditionally associated with joy through remembrance of krishna's leelas
Purifies the heart through devotional poetry
Invokes the fruit of braja bhakti
Kindles divine love (Prema) in the devotee
krishna-karnamrutam-key-verses: Composed by Bilvamangala Thakur, one of the greatest Krishna devotees
Contains the famous Madhurashtakam describing Krishna's all-pervading sweetness
Steeps the listener in Bilvamangala's rapturous love for Krishna
Includes iconic verse describing Krishna's ornaments and beauty
Fills the heart with divine bliss and rasa
krishna-stuti: Traditionally recited for removal of all sorrows and afflictions of the devotee
Invokes the blessing of the blessings of lord krishna in all forms
Twelve verses chanted to dissolve sins and cleanse the soul
Ideal for daily recitation for Krishna devotees
kubera-chalisa: Draws the wealth and abundance guarded by lord of riches Kubera
Lifts poverty and money troubles through the treasurer's grace
Promotes success in business and career advancement
Recited on Dhanteras and Diwali to open Kubera's coffers
Invokes the blessings of all nine types of divine treasures (navnidhi)
kubera-dwadash-nama: Twelve names of Kubera, the celestial treasurer, for wealth
Recited to lift poverty and steady a strained household budget
Attracts the nine treasures (nava-nidhi) to the devotee
Traditionally recited to invoke lakshmi (prosperity) remains stable in the household
Best recited on Dhanteras, Diwali, and every morning
kubera-lakshmi-stotram: Combined prayer to Kubera and Lakshmi for maximum wealth blessings
Recited to clear poverty and steady wavering finances
Invokes the blessing of padma nidhi and shankha nidhi (divine treasures)
Attracts gold, silver, grains, and precious gems
Traditionally recited for removal of debts and financial distress
Ideal for recitation during Dhanteras and Diwali
kubera-pancharatnam: Five-gem hymn to Kubera, guardian of the gods' treasury
Seeks the yaksha-king's grace for wealth and lasting prosperity
Calls on Kubera's watch over the home and its fortunes
Recited to clear money obstacles and grow spiritual merit
Chanted on Diwali and Dhanteras for the treasurer's favour
kubera-stotram: Invokes blessings of Kubera, the divine treasurer of the gods
Pulls in wealth and abundance through the yaksha-king's favour
Frees from debts and financial difficulties
Traditionally recited to invoke stable and lasting prosperity in the household
Invokes the grace of both shankhanihi and padmanidhi treasures
Especially powerful when recited on Dhanteras and Diwali
kurma-stotram: Invokes the stabilizing power of Vishnu's Kurma avatar
Invokes stability and support in life's churning moments
Chanted to clear all sins and gain the tortoise-lord's shelter
Invokes the blessing of brahma-jnana (supreme knowledge)
Sought by devotees as a path to Vishnu's eternal abode
Provides foundation and grounding energy in spiritual practice
lakshmi-ashtakam: Invokes the blessing of all eight types of wealth (ashta lakshmi)
Eight verses sung to burn away sins and negative karma
Invokes material prosperity and spiritual liberation
Traditionally recited for removal of enemies when recited three times daily
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty and brings abundance
Pleases Goddess Mahalakshmi for her eternal grace
lakshmi-ashtottara-shatanamavali: Reciting 108 names of Lakshmi bestows wealth and prosperity
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty, debts, and financial difficulties permanently
Used during Lakshmi Puja, Deepawali, and Friday worship
Invokes all eight forms of lakshmi (ashta lakshmi) blessings
Chanted on Fridays, Diwali, and Sharad Purnima for Mahalakshmi
lakshmi-chalisa: Invokes the blessing of wealth, prosperity, and abundance in life
Recited to drive out poverty and ease money worries
Traditionally associated with peace and harmony in the household
Sung to fulfil both worldly needs and spiritual longings
Invokes the divine grace of goddess lakshmi
Traditionally recited to invoke continuous flow of fortune and good luck
lakshmi-dwadash-nama: Invokes the twelve sacred names of Goddess Lakshmi
Invokes all desired wealth and prosperity when recited at dawn
Traditionally recited to invoke the constant grace of goddess lakshmi
Traditionally associated with auspiciousness and increases happiness
Recited on Diwali and Varalakshmi Vrata to lift poverty
lakshmi-gayatri: Invokes Mahalakshmi for wealth and abundance
Japa believed to settle Lakshmi permanently in the devotee's home
Invokes dhana-dhanya samriddhi — prosperity in wealth and grains
Powerful for Fridays, Diwali, and Varalakshmi Vrata
Two variations covering Vishnu-patni and Kshirasagara aspects
Recited to dispel poverty and steady the household's finances
lakshmi-stuti: Composed by Indra — the king of gods praising Lakshmi
From Vishnu Purana — one of the most authentic Lakshmi prayers
Invokes sarva sampat samriddhi — complete prosperity without doubt
Describes Lakshmi as Sri, Ishvari, Vidya, Medha — all positive qualities
Ideal for Diwali, Fridays, and Varalakshmi Vrata
Recited to banish poverty and grant every form of prosperity
